displayTitle: If It's Not on the Report, It Didn't Happen
kicker: RT LEVEL II // CODES, PROCEDURES, REPORTING

beats.narration: Radiography makes two things: an image, and a record. Four words run the paper side, and they are not interchangeable.

payload.title: THE PAPER SIDE
payload.sub: Code, standard, spec, procedure — and the report that proves them.

beats.narration: The code says when radiography is required and what passes. The ASTM standards say how. A spec can be tighter, never looser.
Follow the procedure as written. Before any film leaves the bench, run parameter verification like a pilot's checklist.
Then the report. Traceability, technique data, a shot map tying every film to its place, findings, disposition, and your signed name.

payload.heading: The report is the verdict
payload.note: FILMS ARE EVIDENCE · THE REPORT IS THE VERDICT

beats.narration: A stranger should reshoot the job and reach your conclusion from the record alone, years later. If it's not on the report, it didn't happen.

payload.sub: The films are evidence — the report is the record.
